svg.text: GENERAL ELECTRICAL SCHEMATIC — REDUCED-SCALE ESP32-S3 SMART ELEVATOR PROTOTYPE (4 FLOORS)

g.text: power rail (colour = ATX wire colour, thick line)
software / network boundary — no physical wiring
junction dot = connected; crossing without dot = not connected
C1–C6 → GPIO CAUTION NOTES box · G1–G6 → GENERAL NOTES box
power flags (net labels): +12 V / +5 V from ATX;
ESP32_3V3 = on-board LDO output (NOT ATX +3.3 V)
COMMON_GND — all ground symbols are one node (ATX black)
Laboratory prototype wiring architecture — not a certified industrial elevator
controller. Scale: none. Sheet 1/1, A4 landscape.
STANDBY — ALWAYS LIVE while mains connected
MAIN RAILS — LIVE ONLY WHILE PS-ON IS HELD LOW

svg.text: +5VSB — standby rail, ALWAYS LIVE → ESP32 5V/VIN (see STANDBY POWER BEHAVIOUR box)
+3.3 V (ATX orange) → KY-024 VCC bus only (G2)

g.text: When ATX main rails are OFF, the ESP32-S3 stays powered from
+5VSB while KY-024 (+3.3 V) and actuator rails are unpowered.
Unpowered sensors leave GPIO inputs floating and may be
back-powered through their outputs. Firmware should treat
sensors as unavailable while main rails are off; protect inputs /
ensure safe pull states (series resistors — design check, not fitted).
central controller — 3.3 V logic, NOT 5 V tolerant (G1)
on-module Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz radio — wireless, no cable
strap pins used: GPIO0, GPIO3, GPIO45, GPIO46
native USB pins used: GPIO19, GPIO20 — see GPIO CAUTION NOTES (C1–C6)
bottom pins = GPIO numbers (labels on wires)
PHYSICAL FLOOR CONFIRMATION — KY-024 HALL-EFFECT SENSORS
VCC = ATX +3.3 V (orange) · DO 3.3 V-safe for direct input
Output polarity must be bench-tested (G4) · debounce in firmware
Route sensor wires away from motor wires (G5)
DOOR END-POSITION CONFIRMATION — SPDT LIMIT SWITCHES
• Recommended NC wiring with pull-up (COM→GND, NC→GPIO); final logic must match firmware.
• Debounce required in firmware or hardware (≥ 20 ms).
• Open-limit AND closed-limit active simultaneously is invalid → raise a fault.
• Door obstruction sensing is NOT included (G6). Door motor stops on limit confirmation (see U3).
• Pins GPIO7 / GPIO15: design assignment — not yet present in firmware v6 (G4).
• Switch contacts carry only 3.3 V pull-up current — no 5 V is injected into ESP32 GPIOs.
SIMULATED TELEMETRY PROXIES — NOT CALIBRATED SENSORS
VMOT — TBD / actuator rail (verify driver supply range)
Driver current per NEMA17; DIP TBD.
VS — actuator rail; 5 V logic from on-board reg (jumper, TBD)
Door dwell starts only after open-limit (S1) confirmation.
SIG → GPIO19 · sinks: ON = LOW; OFF = INPUT_PULLUP (C5)
if 5 V module → level shifter on SDA/SCL (G1)
security block — RFID access control for cabin/hall calls
Verify KY-024 output level during reset;
incorrect level may affect boot mode.
Test with cabin magnet parked at F3.
level; avoid unwanted pull during boot.
pull level when the RFID module is
Buzzer / RC522 RST sit on USB D−/D+.
E-stop / door EN / door IN2 pins are
Markers C1–C6 appear next to the affected
wires/pins on this sheet. All cautions are
design checks to close during the bench
campaign — none is a confirmed fault.
pins and must never be used.
U1 Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz → MQTT (TLS) → Mosquitto broker
→ telemetry bridge → Eclipse Ditto digital twin
wireless link from U1 radio (see note inside U1) — no cable;
commands re-enter U1 over the same MQTT session
Project: Agentic AI-Driven Digital Twin for
Sheet 1/1 (A4 landscape) · Scale NTS · Rev C ·
G1 ESP32-S3 GPIOs are 3.3 V logic — NOT 5 V tolerant; check every module VCC.
G2 Do NOT tie ATX +3.3 V (orange) to ESP32 3V3 LDO output — separate sources, common GND only.
G3 All ground symbols are one COMMON_GND node (ATX black).
G4 KY-024 / SPDT pins are design assignments, not yet in firmware v6; levels pending bench test.
G5 Route sensor/button wiring away from motor and relay
wiring; logic and actuator power separated, one ground.
G6 Door obstruction sensing is not included in this prototype.
